seoTitle: Mount Samson vs Mount Sheridan — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Samson and Mount Sheridan. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Samson vs Mount Sheridan: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Samson and Mount Sheridan.
heading: Mount Samson vs Mount Sheridan — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Samson, Queensland and Mount Sheridan, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Samson has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Mount Sheridan (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Samson or Mount Sheridan safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Samson has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Mount Sheridan scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Samson scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Sheridan scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Samson or Mount Sheridan?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Samson has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
